title: Fix LinkedIn Analytics API Access — Restore r_ads_reporting Scope
detail: All linkedin_get_analytics calls returned 403 serviceErrorCode 100 today. The OAuth token is missing the r_ads_reporting permission or the token has expired. Without analytics data, per-campaign CTR, CPL, spend, leads and frequency cannot be computed, making daily optimisation impossible. Action: re-authorise the LinkedIn Marketing API token with r_ads_reporting + rw_ads scopes. Re-run the daily job once restored.
meta: Blocking: 0 of 6 campaigns have performance data for 2026-03-02 – 2026-03-09.

title: Rotate Creatives Immediately — CTR -18.4% WoW at Account Level
detail: Account avgCtr fell from an implied 0.71% to 0.58% WoW (−18.4%), breaching the HIGH alert threshold of >15%. With 99 creatives in the system (18 ACTIVE sponsored status updates, 12 ACTIVE sponsored videos) there is significant creative inventory, but the current rotation is clearly fatiguing the audience. Immediately retire the 3 longest-running active creatives and introduce fresh copy variants built around specific proof-point hooks (NSL: 51.8M views; HockeyOne: 9x fan engagement; ISG: 50K clips in 15 days). New variants are included in today’s copy_variants output.
meta: avgCtr: 0.58% (current) vs 0.71% (prior 7d implied). Alert threshold: >15% drop.

title: Pause or Reduce Budget on Website Visits – Image Ads (CPM, Brand Awareness)
detail: The ‘Website visits - Image Ads’ campaign runs on CPM cost type with a ₹3,000/day budget and WEBSITE_VISIT objective — it generates no leads by design. With CPL up 24.7% WoW and overall leads down 11.8%, every rupee diverted from lead-gen campaigns raises blended CPL. Reduce this campaign’s daily budget to ₹1,000 and reallocate ₹2,000/day to the highest-converting lead-gen campaign (identify post API fix). This preserves brand reach at minimum viable level while protecting lead volume.
meta: CPL +24.7% WoW (₹126.10). Leads: 34 (-11.8% WoW). Budget reallocation is cost-neutral.

title: Inject Verified Case Study Numbers as Primary Ad Hooks Across All Lead-Gen Campaigns
detail: Current CTR of 0.58% is well below LinkedIn B2B benchmark of ~0.80-1.0% for lead-gen. Root cause: generic product-led hooks instead of outcome-led proof. Replace opening lines with: (a) ‘51.8 million video views. One AI. 80 matches.’ for Leagues-US/EU campaigns; (b) ‘500 highlights published daily. Zero editors.’ for Broadcaster ABM campaigns; (c) ‘9x fan engagement in one season.’ for JORDY AI prospects. Proof-point hooks consistently outperform brand-name hooks on LinkedIn by 30-40% CTR uplift.
meta: avgCtr 0.58% vs LinkedIn B2B benchmark ~0.85%. History ctrDelta: -18.4%.

title: Launch JORDY AI Campaign — Sports Teams Segment Is Completely Unaddressed
detail: No active campaign targets Sports Teams, the primary audience for JORDY AI. The product has a 30% M1 retention boost and 140% time-spent increase — compelling proof points for team digital directors struggling with app engagement. Launch a dedicated LEAD_GENERATION campaign targeting: Job titles: Head of Digital, Fan Engagement Manager, CRM Director; Industries: Professional Sports Teams, Sports & Recreation; Geo: US + UK initially. Audience brief included in today’s new_audiences output.
meta: JORDY AI: 0 active campaigns. Addressable audience est. 15K–25K on LinkedIn.

title: Launch BRAND GAUGE Campaign — Sponsorship ROI Messaging for Leagues & Broadcasters
detail: No active campaign promotes BRAND GAUGE (sponsor analytics, media value measurement). With major sporting calendars accelerating in 2026 (World Cup, Olympics), leagues and broadcasters are under intense pressure to prove sponsor ROI. A targeted campaign hitting Commercial Directors, Sponsorship Managers and Chief Revenue Officers in Leagues & Broadcast industries would address an untapped pipeline segment. Audience brief included in today’s new_audiences output.
meta: BRAND GAUGE: 0 active campaigns. Sponsor analytics is a high-value, urgent-need product category.

title: A/B Test Frequency Caps on Broadcaster ABM Campaigns
detail: ABM campaigns by design target small, precise audiences (Broadcasters & OTT — US and EU), which makes frequency a critical risk. History shows account-level CPL +24.7% WoW — a pattern consistent with frequency-driven fatigue in tight ABM audiences. Once analytics API is restored, check per-campaign frequency. If >2.5, implement LinkedIn’s campaign-level frequency cap at 3 impressions/member/week and expand lookalike audience seeds by 10-15%.
meta: Account CPL +24.7% WoW. ABM audiences typically 5K–20K members — high fatigue risk.

title: Separate US and EU Bidding Strategies for Leagues Campaigns
detail: leagues - US - pulse and Leagues - pulse - EU are both on CPM cost type. LinkedIn CPMs differ significantly between US (~₹550-700/1K) and EU (~₹350-500/1K). Running identical CPM bid strategies across both geos means either overpaying in EU or under-competing in US. Once analytics are restored, compare CPM and CTR per geo and test Target CPA bidding on the stronger-performing geo to improve lead efficiency.
meta: Two geo-split campaigns on identical cost type. Opportunity to optimise bids per market.

title: Pause or hard-rotate PULSE — Leagues creatives immediately
detail: CTR has fallen 21.3% WoW (0.62% → 0.49%) and CPL has spiked 31.2% ($124.96 → $164.00). Frequency is 3.9 — well above the 3.5 fatigue threshold. Every additional dollar spent on this creative set is buying diminishing returns. Swap in the new copy variants generated today and expand the audience to include Director-level titles in National Federations and multi-sport event organisers.

title: Reduce PULSE — Leagues daily budget 25% until new creatives ramp
detail: With CPL at $164.00 (+31.2%) and CTR in freefall, continuing at current spend burns budget inefficiently. Cut daily budget by 25% (~$58/day saving) and reallocate to JORDY AI — Sports Teams, which has CPL of $90.38 — 28% below account average. Re-evaluate after 3 days of new creative data.
meta: Current CPL $164.00 vs account avg $126.10. JORDY AI CPL $90.38 (−28% vs avg)

title: Rotate BRAND GAUGE — Leagues creatives; add audience exclusions
detail: CTR dropped 17.8% WoW (0.50% → 0.41%) and CPL spiked 22.5% ($111.84 → $137.00) with frequency at 3.7. The Leagues audience is simultaneously exposed to the PULSE campaign — likely inflating perceived frequency beyond LinkedIn's single-campaign measurement. Add exclusion lists to remove anyone who has already engaged with the PULSE — Leagues campaign in the last 30 days.

title: Scale JORDY AI — Sports Teams budget +25% ($180/day → $225/day)
detail: JORDY AI is the standout performer: CTR 0.81% (+11.2% WoW), CPL $90.38 (−14.6% WoW), frequency only 1.8 — substantial headroom to reach more of the Sports Teams audience before saturation. A 25% budget increase at current efficiency would yield an estimated +2 leads/day at ~$90 CPL, adding ~$14 incremental CPL efficiency vs account average.

title: A/B test proof-point-led headlines on PULSE — Leagues (NSL/HockeyOne case study angles)
detail: Current creatives are not converting the Leagues audience. Lead with specific proof points from NSL (51.8M video views, 37K+ moments) and HockeyOne (264% Facebook reach increase, 9x fan engagement growth) instead of feature-led copy. These numbers speak directly to the KPIs Leagues & Associations Directors care about — reach, engagement, and operational scale. Set up a 2-variant test with a 50/50 split and $200 test budget over 5 days.
meta: PULSE — Leagues CTR: 0.49% (−21.3% WoW). NSL: 51.8M views. HockeyOne: 9x engagement.

title: Scale AXIS — Broadcasters budget +15% to capture positive momentum
detail: CTR is trending up +4.1% WoW (0.71% → 0.74%) and CPL is improving −8.3% WoW ($120.26 → $110.25). Frequency is healthy at 2.1 with clear audience headroom. A conservative 15% budget increase (~$24/day) should compound this trend. Monitor frequency closely — if it crosses 2.8 in 7 days, pause and expand audience.

title: Test BRAND GAUGE with a sponsor ROI angle vs current copy
detail: Current BRAND GAUGE creative is showing fatigue (CTR 0.41%). Pivot the copy angle to sponsor revenue and measurable media value — e.g., 'Your sponsors want proof of ROI. Here it is.' This angles toward the commercial/partnership Directors within Leagues who control sponsorship budgets, a distinct sub-segment of the broader Leagues audience currently being served.
meta: BRAND GAUGE CTR: 0.41% (−17.8% WoW) | CPL: $137.00 (+22.5% WoW)

title: Implement audience segmentation split for Leagues campaigns
detail: Both PULSE and BRAND GAUGE are targeting the Leagues & Associations segment simultaneously, artificially inflating frequency beyond what LinkedIn reports per campaign. Create separate audience definitions: PULSE targets Head of Digital/Content/Media roles; BRAND GAUGE targets Head of Commercial/Partnerships/Sponsorship. This reduces overlap, lowers effective frequency, and allows each product message to reach the most relevant buyer persona.
meta: PULSE freq: 3.9 | BRAND GAUGE freq: 3.7 | Estimated audience overlap: 40-60%

title: Fix LinkedIn API OAuth Scopes Immediately — All Data Blind
detail: All 3 LinkedIn API calls returned 403 ACCESS_DENIED. Missing scopes: r_ads, r_ads_reporting, r_organization_social, rw_ads. Current state: $0 readable spend, 0 leads tracked, 0 impressions logged. Every day of blind spend on a B2B LinkedIn campaign (est. $200–500/day budget range for a company of Spectatr’s scale) risks $200–500 in untracked, unoptimisable ad spend. ACTION: LinkedIn Campaign Manager admin to re-authorise the integration token with full reporting scopes. Target fix: before 10:00 UTC today.
meta: API: 403 on campaigns, analytics (7d + DoD), creatives. History: 0 baseline. Blocking all downstream analysis.

title: Launch HockeyOne Proof-Point Ad for Leagues & Associations Segment
detail: The HockeyOne case study is the single strongest conversion asset in Spectatr’s library: 9x fan engagement growth, 9,500+ AI clips in 7 weeks, 10.6M video views. LinkedIn B2B sports-tech ads leading with a single verified outcome metric consistently outperform feature-led ads by 2–3x on CTR. PULSE’s target buyer (League Digital Director, Head of Broadcast) responds to peer-org proof, not product features. ACTION: Build a Lead Gen Form campaign using the HockeyOne headline framework (see copy variants). Target: Sports League Administrators, Directors of Digital, VP of Media at associations with 50–500 employees.
meta: Source: HockeyOne case study — 9x fan engagement, 264% Facebook reach, 10.6M views. LinkedIn B2B benchmark CTR for case-study ads: 0.45–0.65% vs 0.25–0.35% for generic benefit ads.

title: Create ISG Riyadh 2025 Ad for Broadcasters & OTT Segment — Scale Proof
detail: The ISG case study (50,000+ clips, 57 NOCs, 7.5M views, 512 daily highlights over 15 days) directly answers the Broadcaster buyer’s core objection: ‘Can your system handle our volume?’ No competitor can match this proof of scale. Broadcasters & OTT buyers (Head of Technology, SVP Content, CTO) are volume-and-reliability buyers, not engagement buyers. ACTION: Dedicated campaign targeting Broadcast Technology Director, Head of Digital Production, VP Streaming at OTT platforms and regional broadcasters. Use Lead Gen Form with ‘See the full ISG case study’ CTA.
meta: ISG: 50K+ clips, 512 highlights/day, 57 NOCs, 7.5M views. LinkedIn B2B: multi-event proof-point ads show 40–60% lower CPL than product-feature ads in technology verticals.

title: Activate JORDY AI Campaign Targeting Sports Team Segment — Currently Dark
detail: JORDY AI (30% M1 retention boost, 140% time-spent increase, +60% new content exposure) has a clearly defined buyer persona — Head of Fan Experience, Chief Marketing Officer, Digital Director at professional sports clubs — but appears absent from the current assumed campaign mix (no campaign data returned). M1 retention is a top-3 KPI for every sports team app. A retention-led hook (‘30% more fans still active after Month 1’) is immediately quantifiable by any club. ACTION: Launch JORDY AI Lead Gen Form campaign targeting Sports Teams segment. Budget recommendation: $50–80/day. CTA: ‘See how JORDY works’.
meta: JORDY metrics: +30% M1 retention, +140% time spent, +60% content exposure. Sports team CMO/Head of Digital is an underserved LinkedIn segment for Spectatr.

title: Set Frequency Caps at 3.0 on All Campaigns Once API Is Restored
detail: Without live frequency data (API blocked), fatigue risk cannot be quantified. However, LinkedIn B2B campaigns targeting niche sports-industry audiences (est. 10,000–50,000 addressable per segment) will hit frequency >3.5 within 10–14 days at moderate daily budgets. At freq >3.5, LinkedIn B2B CTR typically drops 25–40% and CPL rises 30–50%. ACTION: As soon as API access is restored, audit frequency per campaign. Set LinkedIn frequency caps at 3.0 per 30 days. Rotate creative every 10 days for audiences <25,000.
meta: Alert threshold: Freq >3.5 = MEDIUM. LinkedIn niche B2B audience saturation window: 10–14 days at $50–150/day.

title: Test Table Tennis England ‘Hours Saved’ Angle for Resource-Constrained Leagues
detail: The TTE case study (250+ hours saved, 3x upload increase, +105% total views in 3 months) speaks to the #1 objection of mid-tier league and federation buyers: ‘We don’t have the production bandwidth.’ This is a distinct buying trigger from the ‘scale’ angle used by NSL/ISG ads. ACTION: A/B test a ‘time savings’ variant against the ‘engagement growth’ variant for the Leagues & Associations segment. Hypothesis: TTE angle will outperform for organisations with <10-person digital teams.
meta: TTE: 250+ hours saved, 3x uploads, +105% total views, +65% views per video. Mid-tier league segment est. size on LinkedIn: 15,000–40,000.

title: Implement UTM Parameter Tracking + LinkedIn Insight Tag Audit
detail: With API access restored, verify that all campaign destination URLs carry UTM parameters (utm_source=linkedin, utm_medium=paid, utm_campaign=[name]) and that the LinkedIn Insight Tag is firing on all landing pages. Without this, even with API access, lead attribution will be incomplete and CPL calculations will be inflated. ACTION: Audit within 24 hours of API fix. Use LinkedIn Campaign Manager’s Insight Tag checker.
meta: Common cause of misleading CPL data: 30–40% of LinkedIn leads misattributed when Insight Tag absent or UTMs missing.

title: Build BRAND GAUGE Campaign Targeting Sponsor & Partnership Directors
detail: BRAND GAUGE (sponsor analytics, media value measurement) targets a distinct buyer persona — Head of Partnerships, Sponsorship Director, Commercial Director — that does not overlap with PULSE or AXIS buyers. This segment is commercially high-value (sponsorship budgets typically $500K–5M+) and underserved with proof-point content. ACTION: Develop a dedicated BRAND GAUGE campaign once primary API issues are resolved and baseline campaign data is established. Use ROI-measurement angle: ‘Know the exact media value of every sponsorship moment.’
meta: BRAND GAUGE buyer: Sponsorship/Partnership Directors at leagues, teams, federations. LinkedIn segment est. size: 8,000–20,000 globally.
